---
title: "Where Stress Holds Northampton Cyclists Back"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: custom
    toc: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r setup}
library(sf)
library(tidyverse)
library(mapgl)
library(here)

flows <- read_sf(here("data/stress_flows.gpkg"), layer = "stress_flows") |>
  mutate(score = pmax(-coalesce(flow_delta, 0), coalesce(flow_lts, 0)))

hex_centers  <- read_sf(here("data/stress_flows.gpkg"), 
                        layer = "hex_centers")

convenience_names = c("Jim's Variety & Package Store",
                      "Pride", "Zee-Mart", "Sandri", 
                      "Singh Brothers' Leeds Sunoco")
destinations <- read_sf(here::here("data/destinations.gpkg")) |> 
  bind_rows(read_sf(here::here("data/food_stores.gpkg")) |> 
              filter(name %in% convenience_names))

schools <- read_sf(here("data/schools.gpkg")) |>
  filter(is_public)
```

```{=html}
<style>
  *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }

  body { margin: 0; padding: 0; font-family: Georgia, serif; background: #fafafa; }

  #page-title {
    padding: 4rem 3rem 3rem;
    max-width: 640px;
    margin: 0 auto;
  }
  #page-title h1 {
    font-size: 2.2rem;
    line-height: 1.25;
    color: #1a1a2e;
    margin-bottom: 1rem;
  }
  #page-title p {
    font-size: 1.1rem;
    line-height: 1.75;
    color: #555;
  }

  #story-wrapper {
    display: flex;
    align-items: flex-start;
    width: 100%;
  }

  #map-panel {
    position: sticky;
    top: 0;
    width: 58%;
    height: 100vh;
    flex-shrink: 0;
    order: 2;
  }

  #text-panel {
    width: 42%;
    padding: 0 2.5rem 60vh;
    order: 1;
  }

  .story-section {
    min-height: 85vh;
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    justify-content: center;
    padding: 3rem 0;
    border-bottom: 1px solid #e8e8e8;
  }
  .story-section:last-child { border-bottom: none; }

  .story-section h2 {
    font-size: 1.35rem;
    margin: 0 0 0.85rem;
    color: #1a1a2e;
    font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
    font-weight: 600;
  }
  .story-section p {
    font-size: 1rem;
    line-height: 1.8;
    color: #444;
    margin: 0 0 0.8rem;
  }
  .story-section p:last-child { margin-bottom: 0; }

  #methodology {
    max-width: 720px;
    margin: 4rem auto;
    padding: 2.5rem 3rem;
    background: #fff;
    border-top: 3px solid #1a1a2e;
  }
  #methodology h2 {
    font-size: 1.4rem;
    margin-bottom: 1.2rem;
    font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;
    color: #1a1a2e;
  }
  #methodology p, #methodology ol {
    font-size: 0.97rem;
    line-height: 1.8;
    color: #444;
  }
  #methodology ol { padding-left: 1.4rem; }
  #methodology li { margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }
</style>

<div id="page-title">
  <h1>Where Stress Holds Northampton Cyclists Back</h1>
  <p>Not all streets feel safe to bike on. This story maps the gap between where
  cyclists could go and where stress lets them comfortably travel — and shows
  which streets, if made safer, would make the biggest difference.</p>
  <p style="font-size:0.9rem; color:#888;">Scroll to explore &darr;</p>
</div>

<div id="story-wrapper">
  <div id="map-panel">
```

```{r map}
maplibre(
  style    = carto_style("positron"),
  elementId = "main-map",
  height   = "100vh"
) |>
  fit_bounds(unname(st_bbox(hex_centers)), padding = 40) |>
  add_navigation_control(show_compass = FALSE, position = "top-right") |>
  # Population hex centers (initially visible)
  add_source("src-hex", hex_centers) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    "hex-centers", source = "src-hex",
    circle_color = interpolate(
      column = "people_count",
      values = c(0, 200, 400),
      stops  = c("#fee5d9", "#fc4e2a", "#800026")),
    circle_radius = interpolate(
      column = "people_count",
      values = c(0, 400),
      stops  = c(4, 8)),
    circle_opacity   = 0.7,
    circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff",
    circle_stroke_width = 1,
    visibility = "visible",
    tooltip = "people_count"
  ) |>
  # Schools (initially hidden, opacity starts at 0)
  add_source("src-schools", schools) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    "schools-layer", source = "src-schools",
    circle_color        = "#c0392b",
    circle_radius       = 9,
    circle_opacity      = 0,
    circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff",
    circle_stroke_width = 2,
    visibility = "none",
    tooltip = "name"
  ) |>
  # Destinations (initially hidden)
  add_source("src-dest", destinations) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    "destinations-layer", source = "src-dest",
    circle_color        = "#27ae60",
    circle_radius       = 9,
    circle_opacity      = 0,
    circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff",
    circle_stroke_width = 2,
    visibility = "none",
    tooltip = "name"
  ) |>
  # Small school/destination markers for stress maps (same sources, smaller radius)
  add_circle_layer(
    "schools-small", source = "src-schools",
    circle_color        = "#c0392b",
    circle_radius       = 5,
    circle_opacity      = 0,
    circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff",
    circle_stroke_width = 1,
    visibility = "none",
    tooltip = "name"
  ) |>
  add_circle_layer(
    "destinations-small", source = "src-dest",
    circle_color        = "#27ae60",
    circle_radius       = 5,
    circle_opacity      = 0,
    circle_stroke_color = "#ffffff",
    circle_stroke_width = 1,
    visibility = "none",
    tooltip = "name"
  ) |>
  # Unweighted flow (initially hidden)
  add_source("src-unweighted", flows |> filter(flow_unweighted > 0)) |>
  add_line_layer(
    "unweighted-flow", source = "src-unweighted",
    line_color = interpolate(
      column = "flow_unweighted",
      values = c(100, 1500, 30000),
      stops  = c("#fee5d9", "#fc4e2a", "#800026")),
    line_width = interpolate(
      column = "flow_unweighted",
      values = c(100, 30000),
      stops  = c(1, 6)),
    line_opacity = 0,
    visibility   = "none"
  ) |>
  # LTS-weighted flow (initially hidden)
  add_source("src-weighted", flows |> filter(flow_lts > 0)) |>
  add_line_layer(
    "weighted-flow", source = "src-weighted",
    line_color = interpolate(
      column = "flow_lts",
      values = c(100, 2000, 40000),
      stops  = c("#d1e5f0", "#4393c3", "#053061")),
    line_width = interpolate(
      column = "flow_lts",
      values = c(100, 40000),
      stops  = c(1, 6)),
    line_opacity = 0,
    visibility   = "none"
  ) |>
  # Unavoidable high-stress segments (initially hidden)
  add_source("src-unavoidable", 
             flows |> filter(flow_lts >= 350, LTS >= 3)) |>
  add_line_layer(
    "unavoidable-stress", source = "src-unavoidable",
    line_color = "#c0392b",
    line_width = interpolate(
      column = "flow_lts",
      values = c(100, 25000),
      stops  = c(1.5, 7)),
    line_opacity = 0,
    visibility   = "none"
  ) |>
  # Avoided-or-unavoidable (initially hidden)
  add_source("src-avoided",
    flows |> filter((flow_delta < 0 | flow_lts > 0) & LTS >= 3, score>=4000)) |>
  add_line_layer(
    "avoided-unavoidable", source = "src-avoided",
    line_color = interpolate(
      column = "score",
      values = c(4000, 12000, 32000),
      stops  = c("#fee090", "#f46d43", "#a50026")),
    line_width   = 2.5,
    line_opacity = 0,
    visibility   = "none"
  )
```

```{=html}
  </div><!-- /map-panel -->

  <div id="text-panel">

    <section class="story-section" data-section="intro">
      <h2>Where People Live</h2>
      <p>For this study, Northampton 
      was divided into a grid of hexagonal cells, each about
      500 feet (150 meters) on a side — roughly the size of a city block. Within
      each cell, we found the population-weighted center: the point closest to
      where most residents actually live.</p>
      <p>Each circle on this map represents one of those cells, sized and colored
      by the number of residents. Large red circles are densely populated; small
      pale dots have fewer residents. These are the starting points for every
      trip in the analysis.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="schools">
      <h2>Getting Kids to School</h2>
      <p>Seven public schools (shown in red) serve Northampton's children.
      The daily school commute is one of the most revealing tests of a city's
      cycling infrastructure: it's a trip families make repeatedly, in all
      weather, with children in tow.</p>
      <p>Whether neighborhoods can reach their schools by bike — comfortably,
      not just theoretically — shapes whether families ever try.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="destinations">
      <h2>Everyday Destinations</h2>
      <p>Beyond schools, people travel to libraries, the YMCA, grocery stores,
      parks, downtown shops, and medical facilities (shown in green). These are
      the places a connected cycling network should reliably reach.</p>
      <p>Together, schools and other destinations give us roughly 9,000
      origin-destination pairs — one for every population cell and
      destination combination.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="unweighted">
      <h2>The Shortest Routes</h2>
      <p>If cyclists could ignore traffic stress entirely and always take the
      shortest route, this is where bicycle traffic would concentrate. Bright red
      lines carry the most riders; pale lines carry fewer.</p>
      <p>Routes spread across the full network — including high-speed and
      high-traffic roads that many cyclists do not feel safe using.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="weighted">
      <h2>When Comfort Matters</h2>
      <p>Now we penalize high-stress streets, routing cyclists onto calmer
      alternatives even when they're longer. The network shifts: some streets gain
      traffic as low-stress alternatives, while high-stress roads lose riders.</p>
      <p>Blue lines show where cyclists actually want to go when the route
      respects their comfort. The thickest corridors are Northampton's true
      cycling backbone.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="unavoidable">
      <h2>Stress You Can't Escape</h2>
      <p>Even when routing avoids stress, some high-stress streets remain. These
      red segments still carry significant bicycle traffic in the comfort-aware
      scenario — they're simply unavoidable.</p>
      <p>Often these are bridge crossings, key intersections, or connector streets
      with no parallel alternative. Improving just a handful of these segments
      could unlock much safer routes for thousands of trips.</p>
    </section>

    <section class="story-section" data-section="avoided">
      <h2>Streets That Matter Most</h2>
      <p>Combining both signals: streets that cyclists actively detour around
      (they were used on shortest routes but dropped when comfort mattered) and
      streets that remain unavoidable despite their stress. The score reflects the
      magnitude of avoidance or unavoidable use.</p>
      <p>These are Northampton's most critical cycling infrastructure needs —
      the places where investment would have the largest effect on ridership.</p>
    </section>

  </div><!-- /text-panel -->
</div><!-- /story-wrapper -->

<div id="methodology">
  <h2>How This Map Was Made</h2>
  <p>Every street in Northampton has been rated for <strong>Level of Traffic
  Stress (LTS)</strong> — a measure of how safe and comfortable it feels to
  bike. LTS 1 streets are comfortable for almost anyone, including children and
  seniors. LTS 4 streets are high-speed or high-traffic roads suitable only for
  the most experienced riders.</p>
  <p>Using residential address data, we overlaid a grid of hexagonal cells —
  each about 500 feet (150 meters) on a side — on Northampton and found the
  population-weighted center of each occupied cell. We then identified seven
  public schools and dozens of everyday destinations across the city.</p>
  <p>For each of the origin-destination pairs, we
  calculated two routes:</p>
  <ol>
    <li><strong>The shortest route by distance</strong> — as if cyclists could
    ignore stress entirely.</li>
    <li><strong>The least-stressful route</strong> — which strongly penalizes
    high-stress segments, pushing cyclists onto calmer alternatives even when
    they're longer.</li>
  </ol>
  <p>By comparing these routes, we can see which streets carry the most bicycle
  traffic, which streets people go out of their way to avoid, and which streets —
  if made safer — would collectively save cyclists the most extra distance.</p>
  <p style="font-size:0.85rem; color:#888; margin-top:1.5rem;">
  Analysis and mapping: Kent S Johnson.<br>
  Level of Traffic Stress criteria: <a href="https://peterfurth.sites.northeastern.edu/2014/05/21/criteria-for-level-of-traffic-stress/" target="_blank">Peter Furth, Northeastern University</a>.<br>
  Data: OpenStreetMap street network · municipal address data · school district boundaries.<br>
  Analysis: R / flownet package.
  </p>
</div>

<script>
(function () {
  // Target opacities for each layer when fully visible
  var layerOpacities = {
    'hex-centers':          0.7,
    'schools-layer':        0.9,
    'destinations-layer':   0.9,
    'schools-small':        0.8,
    'destinations-small':   0.8,
    'unweighted-flow':      0.8,
    'weighted-flow':        0.8,
    'unavoidable-stress':   0.9,
    'avoided-unavoidable':  0.85
  };

  // Paint property name for opacity, keyed by layer type
  function opacityProp(map, layerId) {
    var layer = map.getLayer(layerId);
    return layer ? layer.type + '-opacity' : null;
  }

  // Smooth opacity animation using requestAnimationFrame
  function animateOpacity(map, layerId, from, to, duration, onComplete) {
    var prop = opacityProp(map, layerId);
    if (!prop) return;
    var start = null;
    function step(ts) {
      if (!start) start = ts;
      var t = Math.min((ts - start) / duration, 1);
      var eased = t < 0.5 ? 2*t*t : -1 + (4 - 2*t)*t; // ease-in-out
      map.setPaintProperty(layerId, prop, from + (to - from) * eased);
      if (t < 1) {
        requestAnimationFrame(step);
      } else if (onComplete) {
        onComplete();
      }
    }
    requestAnimationFrame(step);
  }

  function showLayer(map, layerId) {
    map.setLayoutProperty(layerId, 'visibility', 'visible');
    animateOpacity(map, layerId, 0, layerOpacities[layerId], 450, null);
  }

  function hideLayer(map, layerId) {
    var prop = opacityProp(map, layerId);
    if (!prop) return;
    var current = map.getPaintProperty(layerId, prop) || 0;
    animateOpacity(map, layerId, current, 0, 450, function () {
      map.setLayoutProperty(layerId, 'visibility', 'none');
    });
  }

  // Which layers are shown/hidden for each scroll section
  var ALL_LAYERS = ['hex-centers', 'schools-layer', 'destinations-layer',
                    'schools-small', 'destinations-small', 'unweighted-flow',
                    'weighted-flow', 'unavoidable-stress', 'avoided-unavoidable'];

  function sectionConfig(show) {
    return { show: show, hide: ALL_LAYERS.filter(function(l) { return show.indexOf(l) === -1; }) };
  }

  var sections = {
    'intro':       sectionConfig(['hex-centers']),
    'schools':     sectionConfig(['hex-centers', 'schools-layer']),
    'destinations':sectionConfig(['hex-centers', 'schools-layer', 'destinations-layer']),
    'unweighted':  sectionConfig(['unweighted-flow', 'schools-small', 'destinations-small']),
    'weighted':    sectionConfig(['weighted-flow',   'schools-small', 'destinations-small']),
    'unavoidable': sectionConfig(['unavoidable-stress', 'schools-small', 'destinations-small']),
    'avoided':     sectionConfig(['avoided-unavoidable', 'schools-small', 'destinations-small'])
  };

  function activateSection(map, sectionId) {
    var cfg = sections[sectionId];
    if (!cfg) return;
    cfg.show.forEach(function (l) { showLayer(map, l); });
    cfg.hide.forEach(function (l) { hideLayer(map, l); });
  }

  function setupObserver(map) {
    var activeSections = new Set();
    var currentSection = null;

    var observer = new IntersectionObserver(function (entries) {
      entries.forEach(function (entry) {
        if (entry.isIntersecting) {
          activeSections.add(entry.target);
        } else {
          activeSections.delete(entry.target);
        }
      });

      if (activeSections.size === 0) return;

      // Pick the section whose center is closest to the viewport midpoint.
      var viewMid = window.innerHeight / 2;
      var best = null, bestDist = Infinity;
      activeSections.forEach(function (el) {
        var rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
        var dist = Math.abs(rect.top + rect.height / 2 - viewMid);
        if (dist < bestDist) { bestDist = dist; best = el; }
      });

      if (best && best.dataset.section !== currentSection) {
        currentSection = best.dataset.section;
        activateSection(map, currentSection);
      }
    }, { rootMargin: '-30% 0px -30% 0px', threshold: 0 });

    document.querySelectorAll('.story-section[data-section]').forEach(function (el) {
      observer.observe(el);
    });
  }

  // Poll for the mapgl widget instance (HTMLWidgets initialises asynchronously)
  function waitForMap(callback) {
    var attempts = 0;
    var interval = setInterval(function () {
      attempts++;
      var widgets = window.HTMLWidgets
        ? document.querySelectorAll('.html-widget.maplibregl')
        : [];
      var instance;
      for (var i = 0; i < widgets.length; i++) {
        var candidate = HTMLWidgets.getInstance(widgets[i]);
        if (candidate && candidate.getMap && candidate.getMap()) {
          instance = candidate;
          break;
        }
      }
      if (instance) {
        clearInterval(interval);
        callback(instance.getMap());
      } else if (attempts > 40) {
        clearInterval(interval);
        console.warn('BikeStressScrolly: could not find mapgl map instance');
      }
    }, 250);
  }

  waitForMap(function (map) {
    map.scrollZoom.disable();
    map.on('idle', function onFirstIdle() {
      map.off('idle', onFirstIdle);
      activateSection(map, 'intro');
      setupObserver(map);
    });
  });
})();
</script>
```
